title: data.clone() in put() duplicates secret bytes in non-Zeroizing memory
description: lib.rs:175 and 196: data.clone() copies the BTreeMap<String, ByteString> (containing secret bytes) for the patch body, while the original is reserved for the fallthrough create path. BTreeMap and ByteString do not zeroize. This is a second copy after bytes.to_vec() at line 168. The code could be restructured to avoid the clone: if the patch succeeds, return; if it fails and we need to create, reconstruct from value (which is consumed — so we'd need to clone earlier at a different point). Alternatively, accept that two copies exist in memory briefly during put(). At minimum, document the lifetime of the copies.
